design_philosophy.core_loop: Shoot carnival targets with NES Zapper to earn points; higher difficulty shots (targets near disappearing/breaking) award more points. Lose chances for misses or hitting wrong objects; gain lives by collecting diamonds.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward shooting: delaying shots for higher point values increases risk of target disappearing or accidentally hitting protected characters (dog, people, parrot).
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Carnival trick shooting performer—demonstrate precision and timing to achieve high scores across themed shooting galleries.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Nintendo's opinionated design: pure arcade-style score-chasing mechanics with no narrative progression; gameplay stripped to essential point-based feedback loop. Difficulty escalates through target complexity (multiple characters, moving obstacles, varying point zones) rather than enemy patterns or bosses. Carnival theme provides context but never interrupts mechanical flow.

difficulty.lives_system: Starts with 10 chances. One chance lost per miss or hitting wrong target (dog, people, parrot, non-target objects). Game over when chances depleted.
difficulty.continues_system: Extra chances earned by collecting diamonds in regular game modes. In Fun Follies mode, diamonds unlock slot machine with winning line combinations for up to 15 additional chances (requires at least one diamond).
difficulty.checkpointing: None; stage-to-stage progression within play session. Fun Follies sequences through all five stage types in single session.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited agency; difficulty determined by game design (stage layout, target speed, protected character placement). No explicit difficulty settings documented. Risk-reward timing choice (shoot early for safety vs. late for points) provides implicit difficulty modulation.

progression.upgrade_system: Diamonds function as meta-currency: collecting them in individual game modes provides visual indicator of performance, and they unlock better slot machine odds in Fun Follies finale. Not traditional upgrades but progression-marking collectibles.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Five game modes unlock sequentially through play: Balloon Saloon → Flying Saucers → Window Pains → Fun Follies (combines first three + unlocks Trixie's Shot and Bill's Thrills stages). Slot machine access requires diamonds collected in prior stages.
replayability_hooks: Four distinct game mode types with different mechanics and character interactions
Score-chasing design encourages replays to beat high scores
Risk-reward timing in every shot creates varied execution paths
Diamond collection unlocks better odds in slot machine finale
Ascending stage difficulty in Fun Follies mode

world_building_techniques: Carnival setting implied through game mode themes (Balloon Saloon, Flying Saucers, Window Pains, Fun Follies)
Character cameos establish setting context: Barker Bill and assistant Trixie as carnival workers, parrot as comedic obstacle, duck from Duck Hunt as recurring easter egg
Slot machine in Fun Follies reinforces carnival aesthetic as climactic element

environmental_storytelling: Carnival game show setting with audience-implied presence
Barker Bill as carnival authority figure; Trixie as assistant performer
Protected characters (dog, people, parrot) suggest safety rules within performative space
Escalating stage difficulty (Bill's Thrills as climax) mirrors traditional carnival game progression
